1.name: Core
1.description: Atomic/molecule UI primitives reusable across any feature
1.criteria: Is this component directly importable by page developers and reusable across any feature or page?

2.name: Composite
2.criteria: Does this component compose multiple Core components into a page-level pattern (layout, table, navigation)?

3.name: Domain
3.criteria: Is this component only meaningful within a specific business domain (e.g., ad scheduling, video creative)?

4.name: Internal
4.criteria: Is this component used internally by other components and not directly imported by page developers?

categories.name: Form Inputs (v1)
categories.description: All form components use Formik via useField(name). Must be used inside a <Formik> context.

components.name: MCFormTextInput
components.description: Text input with Formik integration. Shows error after touch.
components.shortDescription: Single-line text input with Formik binding
whenToUse: Single-line text input inside Formik forms
When field-level validation with error display is needed
Text fields with label, tooltip, and hint support
Readonly text display within a form context
doNotUse: Outside <Formik> context — use MCSingleTextInput instead
For multi-line text — use MCFormTextArea
For numeric input — use MCFormNumberInput
components.example:
import MCFormTextInput from '@msm-portal/common/component/form/v1/input/MCFormTextInput';

<MCFormTextInput
  name="title"
  fieldLabel="Campaign Title"
  required
  tooltip="Max 100 characters"
  hint="Used as the display name"
/>

components.name: MCFormTextArea
components.description: Multi-line text input with Formik integration.
components.shortDescription: Multi-line text input with Formik binding
whenToUse: Multi-line text input inside Formik forms (descriptions, notes, comments)
When users need to enter paragraphs or freeform text
Text fields requiring more than one line of input
doNotUse: Outside <Formik> context — use MCSingleTextArea instead
For single-line inputs — use MCFormTextInput
For rich text editing — this is plain text only

components.name: MCFormNumberInput
components.description: Numeric input with Formik integration. Converts string to parseFloat internally.
whenToUse: Numeric value entry inside Formik forms (budget, count, percentage)
When the value must be stored as a number rather than a string
Numeric fields with automatic parseFloat conversion
doNotUse: Outside <Formik> context
For phone numbers or zip codes — use MCFormTextInput (these are string-formatted)
For frequency caps with optional toggle — use MCFormOptionalFrequencyInput

components.name: MCFormCheckBox
components.description: Checkbox with Formik integration.
whenToUse: Boolean on/off choices inside Formik forms (agree to terms, opt-in)
Multi-select from a small set of options using multiple checkboxes
When non-exclusive selection is needed within a form
doNotUse: Outside <Formik> context
For mutually exclusive options — use MCFormRadioGroup
For immediate toggle actions — use MCFormSwitchInput
components.example: <MCFormCheckBox name="agreeToTerms" fieldLabel="I agree to terms" required />

components.name: MCFormSwitchInput
components.description: Toggle switch with Formik integration.
whenToUse: Immediate binary toggle actions inside Formik forms (enable/disable feature)
When the state change is conceptually 'on/off' rather than 'checked/unchecked'
doNotUse: Outside <Formik> context
Inside form submission flows where checked state matters — use MCFormCheckBox instead
For multi-option selection — use MCFormCheckBox or MCFormMultiRichSelect

components.name: MCFormRadioGroup
components.description: Radio button group with Formik integration.
components.shortDescription: Radio button group with Formik binding
whenToUse: Mutually exclusive choice from 2-5 options inside Formik forms
When all options should be visible at once (not hidden in a dropdown)
Selection fields like payment type, plan tier with few options
doNotUse: Outside <Formik> context
For more than 5 options — use MCFormSingleRichSelect
For non-exclusive choices — use MCFormCheckBox
components.example:
<MCFormRadioGroup
  name="paymentType"
  fieldLabel="Payment Type"
  direction="row"
  options={[
    { label: 'CPC', value: 'cpc' },
    { label: 'CPM', value: 'cpm' },
  ]}
/>

components.name: MCFormSingleRichSelect
components.description: Single-select dropdown with Formik integration. Generic over value type.
whenToUse: Single selection from 5+ options inside Formik forms
Dropdown selection for status, category, or entity type fields
When options are too many to display as radio buttons
doNotUse: Outside <Formik> context
For 2-3 options — use MCFormRadioGroup for better visibility
For multiple selection — use MCFormMultiRichSelect

components.name: MCFormMultiRichSelect
components.description: Multi-select dropdown with Formik integration. Generic over value type.
whenToUse: Multiple selection from a large option set inside Formik forms
Tag-style selection where users pick several values from a dropdown
When selected values need to be displayed as chips or a count
doNotUse: Outside <Formik> context
For single selection — use MCFormSingleRichSelect
For inline chip display with fewer options — use MCFormInlineChipRichSelect

components.name: MCFormCardSelect
components.description: Card-based visual selector with Formik integration.
components.shortDescription: Card-based visual selector with Formik binding
whenToUse: Visual selection with descriptions inside Formik forms (plan type, template)
When each option needs a label and description for clarity
Mutually exclusive choice where visual emphasis aids decision-making
doNotUse: Outside <Formik> context
For simple text-only options — use MCFormRadioGroup
For more than 6 options — use MCFormSingleRichSelect dropdown
components.example:
<MCFormCardSelect
  name="planType"
  fieldLabel="Plan Type"
  options={[
    { label: 'Basic', value: 'basic', description: 'Entry level plan' },
  ]}
/>

components.name: MCFormInlineChipRichSelect
components.description: Chip-style multi-select rendered inline. Selected values appear as removable chips.
whenToUse: Multi-select where selected values should be visible as inline chips
When space is limited and a full dropdown would be too heavy
Inline tag-like selection for small to medium option sets (up to 10)
doNotUse: Outside <Formik> context
For single selection — use MCFormSingleRichSelect
For more than 10 options — use MCFormMultiRichSelect with dropdown

components.name: MCFormDateRangePicker
components.description: Date range picker with Formik integration.
components.shortDescription: Date range picker with Formik binding
whenToUse: Date range selection inside Formik forms (campaign periods, report date ranges)
When users need to pick a start and end date without time
doNotUse: Outside <Formik> context
For date + time ranges — use MCFormDateTimeRangePicker
For single date selection — check if a single date picker exists

components.name: MCFormDateTimeRangePicker
components.description: Date + time range picker with Formik integration. Combines date and time selection for start/end range.
components.shortDescription: DateTime range picker with Formik binding
whenToUse: Scheduling with both date and time inside Formik forms (campaign scheduling, event windows)
When users need to select start/end date AND start/end time
Time-precise range selection for ad delivery or scheduling
doNotUse: Outside <Formik> context
For date-only ranges — use MCFormDateRangePicker
For single datetime selection — check if a single datetime picker exists

components.name: MCFormColorInput
components.description: Color picker with hex input and Formik integration. Shows a color swatch preview and accepts hex color codes.
components.shortDescription: Color picker input with Formik binding
whenToUse: Brand color configuration inside Formik forms (theme customization, branding)
When users need to enter a hex color code with visual preview
doNotUse: Outside <Formik> context
For predefined color selection from a list — use MCFormSingleRichSelect with color swatches
For non-color text input — use MCFormTextInput

components.name: MCFormChipInput
components.description: Text input that creates chips on Enter or comma. Formik integrated. Stores values as string array.
whenToUse: Free-form tag/keyword input inside Formik forms (tags, keywords, email lists)
When users type values and confirm them as chips via Enter or comma
String array fields where values are entered one at a time
doNotUse: Outside <Formik> context
For predefined option selection — use MCFormMultiRichSelect
For single string values — use MCFormTextInput
components.example:
<MCFormChipInput
  name="keywords"
  fieldLabel="Keywords"
  hint="Press Enter or comma to add"
  placeholder="Type a keyword..."
/>

components.name: MCFormWeeklyTimeTablePicker
components.description: Grid-based weekly schedule picker (day × hour slots). Formik integrated. Click or drag to select time slots.
components.shortDescription: Weekly schedule picker with Formik binding
whenToUse: Weekly ad scheduling (dayparting) inside Formik forms
When users need to select multiple day-hour time slots on a grid
Recurring weekly schedule configuration
doNotUse: Outside <Formik> context
For single time slot selection — use a time picker
For date-specific scheduling — use MCFormDateTimeRangePicker
components.example:
<MCFormWeeklyTimeTablePicker
  name="adSchedule"
  fieldLabel="Ad Schedule"
  tooltip="Select hours when ads should run"
/>

components.name: MCFormOptionalFrequencyInput
components.description: Frequency cap input with optional toggle. Formik integrated. Toggle enables/disables the frequency limit.
components.shortDescription: Optional frequency input with Formik binding
whenToUse: Optional frequency capping inside Formik forms (impression cap per user)
When a numeric limit needs an enable/disable toggle
Conditional numeric input that can be toggled on or off
doNotUse: Outside <Formik> context
For required numeric input without toggle — use MCFormNumberInput
For simple boolean toggle without numeric value — use MCFormSwitchInput
components.example:
<MCFormOptionalFrequencyInput
  name="frequencyCap"
  fieldLabel="Frequency Cap"
  tooltip="Limit impressions per user per day"
/>

components.name: MCFormSkippableVideoInput
components.description: Video URL input with skippable option toggle. Formik integrated. Combines URL text input with a skip toggle.
components.shortDescription: Video skip time input with Formik binding
whenToUse: Video ad creative URL input inside Formik forms
When a video URL needs a paired skippable toggle option
Video creative configuration in ad creation flows
doNotUse: Outside <Formik> context
For non-video URLs — use MCFormTextInput
For video upload — this is URL-only, not a file upload component

categories.name: Standalone Inputs
categories.description: Reusable input-like components that are not tied to Formik but are still part of the shared Portal contract.

components.name: MCRadioGroup
components.description: Controlled radio group for non-Formik usage. Composes MCFormField, MCFormFieldLabel, and Moloco radio inputs.
components.shortDescription: Standalone radio group without Formik binding
whenToUse: Mutually exclusive option selection outside Formik forms
Custom controlled filters or settings panels that still need MC form styling
When a field needs label, tooltip, and inline error composition without Formik
doNotUse: Inside <Formik> forms — use MCFormRadioGroup
For multi-select choices — use checkbox-based components
For long option lists — use a select or rich select
components.example:
<MCRadioGroup
  value={selectedStatus}
  options={[{ label: 'Active', value: 'active' }, { label: 'Paused', value: 'paused' }]}
  onChange={setSelectedStatus}
  label="Status"
/>

components.name: MCColorPicker
components.description: Popover-based RGBA color picker with swatch preview, hex display, and optional alpha control.
whenToUse: Color selection outside Formik-managed fields
Places that need a preview swatch plus RGBA/hex feedback
Settings UIs that open a lightweight picker popover instead of a full dialog
doNotUse: Inside standard Formik fields — use MCFormColorInput when possible
For fixed palette choices — use a finite select/grid instead of free color picking
For read-only status color display — render a swatch or label without picker affordance

categories.name: Form Shared
categories.description: Shared building blocks used by form input components. Import from '@msm-portal/common/component/form/shared'.

components.name: MCFormFieldLabel
components.description: Form field label with optional/required state, tooltip, and description.
components.shortDescription: Form field label with tooltip support
whenToUse: Labeling any form input with optional/required indicator
When a field needs a tooltip icon or sub-description below the label
Building custom form fields that need consistent label styling
doNotUse: For standalone headings — use MCFormPanelTitle or MCFormTitle
When using MCFormTextInput or other v1 form components — they render labels internally

components.name: MCFormTooltip
components.description: Info icon that shows a markdown tooltip on hover.
whenToUse: Adding contextual help text to form fields via an info icon
When supplemental information should be available on hover without cluttering the UI
doNotUse: For inline help text — use the hint prop on form inputs instead
For interactive content (links, buttons) — tooltip must be non-interactive
When the tooltip prop on MCFormTextInput/other form components suffices

categories.name: Form Scaffold
categories.description: Structural components that compose the form page layout. Used in patterns.json form patterns. Must be used inside MCFormLayout.

components.name: MCFormPanel
components.description: Container panel for form sections with title and body
whenToUse: Group related form fields into a titled section
Every form page uses at least one MCFormPanel
doNotUse: Outside form context
For non-form content grouping — use a plain styled div

components.name: MCFormPanelTitle
components.description: Title component for MCFormPanel
whenToUse: Title heading inside MCFormPanel
doNotUse: Outside MCFormPanel

components.name: MCFormPanelBody
components.description: Body container inside MCFormPanel, holds field groups
whenToUse: Body content area inside MCFormPanel
doNotUse: Outside MCFormPanel

components.name: MCFormFieldGroup
components.description: Groups related form fields with optional title. Provides consistent spacing between fields.
components.shortDescription: Groups related form fields with spacing
whenToUse: Group related form fields within MCFormPanelBody
Provide consistent vertical spacing between form fields
doNotUse: Outside MCFormPanelBody
For non-form content grouping

components.name: MCFormField
components.description: Wrapper for individual form fields. Provides label, error, and hint layout.
whenToUse: Wrap individual form input components
Provide consistent label + error + hint layout
doNotUse: When using MCFormTextInput (which includes MCFormField internally)

components.name: MCFormTitle
components.description: Title component for the entire form page
whenToUse: Top-level form page title
doNotUse: For panel-level titles — use MCFormPanelTitle

components.name: MCFormBody
components.description: Main body container for form content. Holds MCFormPanels.
components.shortDescription: Main body container for form panels
whenToUse: Container for all MCFormPanels in a form page
doNotUse: Outside MCFormLayout

components.name: MCFormActions
components.description: Action bar at the bottom of a form with submit/cancel buttons
whenToUse: Bottom action bar in form pages with Save/Cancel buttons
doNotUse: Outside MCFormLayout
For non-form action bars

components.name: MCFormFieldError
components.description: Inline error message display for form fields
whenToUse: Display Formik validation error messages below a field
doNotUse: For non-validation messages — use MCFormHint

components.name: MCFormHint
components.description: Helper text below a form field
components.shortDescription: Helper text below a form field
whenToUse: Provide supplementary guidance text below a form field
doNotUse: For error messages — use MCFormFieldError

components.name: MCFormGuideMessage
components.description: Informational guide message within a form panel
whenToUse: Display informational or instructional text within a form section
doNotUse: For field-level hints — use MCFormHint
For error messages — use MCFormFieldError

components.name: MCFormDescription
components.description: Description text below a form field label
whenToUse: Add extended description below a field label
doNotUse: For error messages — use MCFormFieldError
For simple hints — use MCFormHint

components.name: MCFormPortal
components.description: Portal helper that renders form content into the main layout body or root layout body for full-screen experiences.
components.shortDescription: Portal surface for full-screen or overlay form content
whenToUse: Render form surfaces above the standard page body while still staying inside the app layout
Full-screen or overlay form experiences that must mount into root layout containers
doNotUse: For regular in-flow form layout — use MCFormLayout
For general-purpose modal dialogs — use MCCommonDialog
Outside the main/root layout provider tree

categories.name: Form Layout
categories.description: Full-page form layout with header, scrollable body, and footer.

components.name: MCFormLayout
components.description: Full-page form layout. Header: 44px. Body: 860px centered. Footer: border-top.
components.shortDescription: Full form page layout with header and body
whenToUse: Full-page create/edit form layouts with header, scrollable body, and footer
When a form needs a fixed header with breadcrumbs and a fixed footer with action buttons
Entity creation and editing pages (campaigns, ad groups, creatives)
doNotUse: For list or detail pages — use MCContentLayout instead
For inline forms within a page section — use MCFormPanel directly
For dialogs — use MCCommonDialog

components.name: MCButton2
components.description: Primary button. All portal buttons use MCButton2.
whenToUse: Primary, secondary, and text action buttons throughout the portal
Form submit/cancel actions in MCFormActions
Navigation triggers in toolbars and page headers
Any clickable action that is not a link
doNotUse: For contextual action menus — use MCMoreActionsButton
For link-style navigation — use React Router Link or anchor
For icon-only actions without label — ensure aria-label is set

components.name: MCMoreActionsButton
components.description: MCButton2 that opens a popover menu of actions on click.
whenToUse: Contextual actions menu in table rows or card headers
When multiple secondary actions need to be grouped behind a single trigger
Three-dot menu (ellipsis) pattern for entity actions (edit, delete, duplicate)
doNotUse: For primary actions — use MCButton2 directly
For grouped action categories — use MCMoreActionGroupsButton
For navigation — use routing or links
components.example:
<MCMoreActionsButton
  variant="text"
  noPadding
  moreActionItems={[
    { label: 'Edit', icon: 'edit', onClick: handleEdit },
    { label: 'Delete', icon: 'delete', onClick: handleDelete },
  ]}
>
  <MCIcon icon="more" width={16} height={16} />
</MCMoreActionsButton>

components.name: MCMoreActionGroupsButton
components.description: MCButton2 with grouped actions separated by MCDivider.
whenToUse: Action menus with categorized/grouped actions separated by dividers
When actions naturally fall into logical groups (e.g., edit group + danger group)
Complex contextual menus requiring visual separation between action types
doNotUse: For flat action lists without grouping — use MCMoreActionsButton
For primary actions — use MCButton2 directly
For fewer than 3 actions — use MCMoreActionsButton

categories.name: Navigation
categories.description: Primary navigation building blocks used for the app shell and route-level movement.

components.name: MCCollapsibleNavbar
components.description: Collapsible sidebar navigation. Width: 260px when expanded, collapses to icon-only width.
whenToUse: Primary app sidebar navigation for the portal shell
When the application needs a collapsible left-side navigation panel
Top-level route navigation with section grouping
doNotUse: Inside MCContentLayout or MCFormLayout — it sits alongside them at the app root
For secondary in-page navigation — use MCBarTabs
For breadcrumb navigation — use MCContentLayout with showBreadcrumb

components.name: MCNavbarItems
components.description: Renders navigation sections with titles and items. Handles routing and active state highlighting.
whenToUse: Rendering grouped navigation items inside MCCollapsibleNavbar
When navigation needs section titles with grouped route links
Main sidebar menu items with icons and active state highlighting
doNotUse: Outside MCCollapsibleNavbar — it depends on navbar context
For in-page tab navigation — use MCBarTabs
For breadcrumb links — use MCContentLayout breadcrumb
components.example:
<MCNavbarItems
  sections={[
    {
      title: 'Campaign Management',
      items: [
        { label: 'Campaigns', icon: 'targeting', route: MERouteKey.CAMPAIGN_MAIN },
        { label: 'Creatives', icon: 'creative', route: MERouteKey.CREATIVE_MAIN },
      ],
    },
    {
      title: 'Reports',
      items: [
        { label: 'Performance', icon: 'chart', route: MERouteKey.REPORT_MAIN },
      ],
    },
  ]}
/>

categories.name: Feedback & Overlay
categories.description: Dialogs, popovers, and loading feedback components that temporarily sit above page content.

components.name: MCCommonDialog
components.description: Modal dialog with header, content area, and action buttons.
whenToUse: Confirmations, alerts, and small inline forms that overlay the page
When user attention must be captured before proceeding
Content-heavy dialogs with custom body and action buttons
doNotUse: For full-page content or forms — use MCFormLayout
For destructive action confirmations — use MCConfirmDialog (alertdialog role)
For nested dialogs more than 2 levels deep
components.example:
<MCCommonDialog
  open={isOpen}
  onClose={() => setIsOpen(false)}
  title="Confirm Delete"
  showDivider
  actions={
    <>
      <MCButton2 onClick={() => setIsOpen(false)}>Cancel</MCButton2>
      <MCButton2 color="primary" onClick={handleConfirm}>Confirm</MCButton2>
    </>
  }
>
  Are you sure?
</MCCommonDialog>

components.name: MCPopover
components.description: Styled popover. Paper: background.primary, no shadow, border.primary border.
whenToUse: Contextual menus and tooltips anchored to a trigger element
Lightweight overlays that do not need full dialog semantics
Custom dropdown content that is not a standard select
doNotUse: For complex forms or heavy content — use MCCommonDialog
For simple action menus — use MCMoreActionsButton which wraps popover internally
For permanent/persistent content — popover is transient by design

components.name: MCLoader
components.description: Loading spinner. Use while async data is being fetched.
whenToUse: Inline or section-level loading indicators while async data fetches
When a specific section of a page is loading independently
doNotUse: For full-page loading — use MCCircularLoader with fillParent
For less than 300ms loading times — use debounced loading state to avoid flicker

categories.name: Display
categories.description: Shared presentation components for showing content structure, separation, tabs, status, and progress.

components.name: MCAccordion
components.description: Collapsible section with animated expand/collapse. Toggle icon: angle-down (rotates 180° when expanded).
whenToUse: Progressive disclosure of optional or advanced content sections
Collapsible form sections within MCFormPanel (e.g., Advanced Settings)
When page content should be organized into expandable sections
doNotUse: For tab-based content switching — use MCBarTabs
For nested accordions inside accordions — keep hierarchy flat
For always-visible content — just render it directly

components.name: MCBarTabs
components.description: Tab bar with bar indicator.
whenToUse: Content switching within a single page (e.g., Overview / Settings tabs)
When multiple views share the same page context but show different content
Tab navigation that persists active tab in URL search params
doNotUse: For navigation between different pages/routes — use routing
For more than 6 tabs — consider a different navigation pattern
For collapsible sections — use MCAccordion
components.example:
<MCBarTabs
  tabs={[
    { label: 'Overview', active: activeTab === 'overview', onClick: () => setActiveTab('overview') },
    { label: 'Settings', active: activeTab === 'settings', onClick: () => setActiveTab('settings') },
  ]}
/>

components.name: MCDivider
components.description: Horizontal or vertical divider using border.primary.
whenToUse: Visually separating content sections within a page or panel
Between action groups in MCMoreActionGroupsButton menus
Vertical dividers between inline elements using direction='column'
doNotUse: For purely decorative spacing — use margin/padding instead
For form section breaks — use MCFormDivider (form-specific variant)

components.name: MCStatus
components.description: Status badge with icon and label. Color and icon are derived automatically.
whenToUse: Displaying entity lifecycle status (active, paused, draft, etc.)
Status columns in MCReportTable via cell renderers
Detail page headers showing current entity state
doNotUse: For custom status colors or icons — use predefined variants only
For progress indicators — use MCStepper
For boolean on/off state — use MCFormSwitchInput

components.name: MCStepper
components.description: Step indicator for multi-step forms/flows. Uses a children-based pattern with MCStepper.Step sub-components.
whenToUse: Multi-step wizard flows (onboarding, multi-page creation)
Showing progress through a sequential process
When users need to see which step they are on and what comes next
doNotUse: For more than 6 steps — simplify the flow
For non-sequential navigation — use MCBarTabs
For status display — use MCStatus
components.example:
<MCStepper activeStep={currentStep} onActiveStepChange={setCurrentStep}>
  <MCStepper.Step title="Basic Info" />
  <MCStepper.Step title="Targeting" />
  <MCStepper.Step title="Creative" />
  <MCStepper.Step title="Review" />
</MCStepper>

components.name: SCBoldLabel
components.description: Bold label using H_5.fontWeight.
whenToUse: Inline bold labels for key-value displays or section emphasis
When a label needs H_5 font weight without being a heading element
doNotUse: For form field labels — use MCFormFieldLabel
For section titles — use MCFormPanelTitle or MCFormTitle

components.name: SCClickableText
components.description: Clickable underlined text. Uses content.contentAccent color.
whenToUse: Inline clickable text links styled with accent color and underline
When a text element needs to be clickable without being a full button
doNotUse: For primary actions — use MCButton2
For navigation links — use React Router Link
For text button variants — use MCButton2 variant='text'

categories.name: Moloco UI Primitives
categories.description: Used directly from @moloco/moloco-cloud-react-ui without portal wrapping.

components.name: MCIcon
components.description: Core Moloco icon primitive used for rendering MTIcon-based icons across the portal.
components.shortDescription: SVG icon from Moloco icon set
whenToUse: All icon rendering throughout the portal
Button start icons, navigation item icons, status icons
Empty state hero icons and inline decorative icons
doNotUse: For custom SVG icons not in the MTIcon catalog — create a custom component
As the sole interactive element — wrap in a button with aria-label

components.name: MCStack
components.description: Core Moloco flex layout helper for horizontal or vertical stacking with spacing.
whenToUse: Quick flex layout composition for stacking elements vertically or horizontally
Empty state layouts, inline element grouping, spacing between elements
doNotUse: For complex page layouts — use MCContentLayout or MCFormLayout
When styled-components with explicit flex is clearer for the use case

components.name: MCSingleTextInput
components.description: Raw single-line text input primitive intended for controlled usage outside Formik wrappers.
whenToUse: Standalone text input outside Formik context (e.g., search bars, filters)
Building custom controlled inputs not tied to form state
doNotUse: Inside Formik forms — use MCFormTextInput which wraps this with Formik integration
For multi-line text — use MCSingleTextArea

components.name: MCSingleTextArea
components.description: Raw multi-line textarea primitive intended for controlled usage outside Formik wrappers.
whenToUse: Standalone multi-line text input outside Formik context
Building custom controlled textareas not tied to form state
doNotUse: Inside Formik forms — use MCFormTextArea which wraps this with Formik integration
For single-line text — use MCSingleTextInput

components.name: MCTextEllipsis
components.description: Single-line text truncation primitive that applies ellipsis when content overflows.
whenToUse: Truncating long text with ellipsis in constrained-width containers
Table cells, card titles, or any element where text overflow needs to be handled
doNotUse: For multi-line text clamping — use CSS -webkit-line-clamp
When full text must always be visible

components.name: MCMarkdownTooltip
components.description: Tooltip primitive that can render markdown-formatted content for richer help text.
whenToUse: Tooltips that need rich markdown formatting (bold, italic, lists)
Standalone tooltips outside form context
doNotUse: Inside form fields — use the tooltip prop on form components or MCFormTooltip
For interactive popover content — use MCPopover

components.name: MCBarTab
components.description: Low-level bar-tab primitive used internally when composing custom tab bars.
whenToUse: Building custom tab bars when MCBarTabs does not fit the use case
doNotUse: For standard tab navigation — use MCBarTabs which composes this internally
For page-level navigation — use routing

components.name: MCBarTabIndicator
components.description: Low-level visual indicator primitive paired with MCBarTab in custom tab bars.
whenToUse: Custom tab indicator positioning when building custom tab bars with MCBarTab
doNotUse: For standard tab navigation — use MCBarTabs which handles the indicator internally

categories.name: Table
categories.description: Table and tabular-data display components used for reporting and dense list views.

components.name: MCReportTable
components.description: Advanced data table with sorting, pagination, row actions, and typed cell renderers. Built on top of ag-grid or similar table engine.
whenToUse: Data tables for list pages (campaigns, ad groups, creatives, reports)
When sorting, pagination, and row click navigation are needed
Tabular data display with typed cell renderers for money, dates, percentages
doNotUse: For fewer than 3 rows — use a simple list or card layout
For non-tabular data — use MCStack or custom layouts
For form data entry in table format — use MCFormFieldGroup

components.name: MCI18nTable
components.description: Localized wrapper around Moloco MCTable. Injects table labels from i18n so empty, pagination, and page indicator text stay translated consistently.
components.shortDescription: Localized table wrapper with shared labels
whenToUse: General shared table usage when Moloco MCTable labels should follow Portal translations
Dense list or report tables that do not need the heavier MCReportTable wrapper
Any table where the standard empty, pagination, and page-indicator copy should be localized automatically
doNotUse: When page developers need the full report-table contract — use MCReportTable
When custom label text must be overridden per screen — use raw MCTable carefully

components.name: MCTableActionBar
components.description: Horizontal action row that separates left-aligned context controls from right-aligned page or table actions.
whenToUse: Above tables when filters, counters, or bulk context live on the left and actions live on the right
Shared list-page headers that belong to the table region rather than the page title area
doNotUse: For page-level hero actions — use MCContentLayout rightAccessory
For single inline button rows that do not need two-sided alignment

categories.name: Layout
categories.description: Page-level layout components for structuring content areas.

components.name: MCContentLayout
components.description: Standard page layout with breadcrumb, title, and optional right accessory. Used for list pages, detail pages, settings pages.
whenToUse: All list pages, detail pages, and settings pages
When a page needs a header with title, breadcrumb, and optional right accessory
Standard page shell for non-form content
doNotUse: For form/create/edit pages — use MCFormLayout instead
Nested inside MCFormLayout — they are mutually exclusive page shells
For dialog content — use MCCommonDialog

components.name: MCCircularLoader
components.description: Full-page or inline circular loading spinner from Moloco UI.
whenToUse: Full-page loading states while data is being fetched
Container-level loading with fillParent for centered spinner
Initial page load or route transition loading
doNotUse: For inline/section loading — use MCLoader instead
For less than 300ms loading times — use debounced loading state

components.name: MCConfirmDialog
components.description: Confirmation dialog with customizable cancel and confirm buttons. Used for destructive action confirmations.
whenToUse: Destructive action confirmations (delete, discard changes, archive)
When the user must explicitly confirm or cancel before a dangerous operation
Alert-style dialogs requiring immediate user attention
doNotUse: For non-destructive confirmations — use MCCommonDialog
For complex forms inside a dialog — use MCCommonDialog with form content
For informational messages — use toast/snackbar notifications
components.example:
<MCConfirmDialog
  isOpen={confirmOpen}
  title={t('dialog.title')}
  cancelButtonOptions={{
    label: t('dialog.cancel'),
    leftIcon: <MCIcon icon="delete" inheritColor />,
    onClick: handleDiscard,
  }}
  confirmButtonOptions={{
    label: t('dialog.confirm'),
    color: 'primary',
    leftIcon: <MCIcon icon="check" inheritColor />,
    onClick: handleConfirm,
  }}
  onClose={() => setConfirmOpen(false)}
>
  {t('dialog.message')}
</MCConfirmDialog>

categories.name: Empty State
categories.description: Patterns for when there is no data to display.

components.name: EmptyState
components.description: Composition pattern for empty data views. Combine MCStack + MCIcon + text + MCButton2.
whenToUse: When a list, table, or section has zero data to display
First-time user experience when no entities have been created yet
Filtered results that return zero matches
doNotUse: When data exists but is loading — use MCLoader or MCCircularLoader
For error states — use an error message pattern instead
When the empty state is temporary (< 300ms) — show loader instead

categories.name: Library Primitives
categories.description: Additional components from @moloco/moloco-cloud-react-ui used directly without portal wrapping. Read type definitions for full API.

components.name: MCButton
components.description: Legacy button component. Prefer MCButton2 for new code. Still used in some older portal areas.
whenToUse: Only when maintaining existing code that already uses MCButton
Legacy compatibility — all new code should use MCButton2
doNotUse: For any new UI development — use MCButton2 instead
When refactoring legacy code — migrate to MCButton2

components.name: MCBanner
components.description: Notification banner for page-level messages. Supports info, warning, and critical variants.
whenToUse: Page-level informational, warning, or critical messages
Persistent alerts that should remain visible until dismissed
System-wide announcements or deprecation notices
doNotUse: For inline field-level errors — use form error components
For transient toast notifications — use snackbar/toast pattern
For dialog-based alerts — use MCConfirmDialog
components.example:
<MCBanner variant="warning" onClose={handleDismiss}>
  This campaign has limited budget remaining.
</MCBanner>

components.name: MCSearchBar
components.description: Search input with debounce support, loading indicator, and optional border/shadow styling.
whenToUse: Table/list search and filtering
Global or section-level search inputs
When search needs debounced input handling
doNotUse: Inside Formik forms — use MCFormTextInput with search icon
For complex filter UIs — use MCFilter component

components.name: MCTag
components.description: Styled inline tag/label for categorization. Fully customizable via style props.
whenToUse: Inline labels or badges for categorization (e.g., 'Beta', 'New', 'Premium')
Status-like tags in table cells or headers
Visually marking items with a category label
doNotUse: For entity lifecycle status — use MCStatus
For removable chip selections — use MCChip
components.example:
<MCTag backgroundColor="#E3F2FD" fontColor="#1565C0" fontSize="12px" borderRadius="4px" padding="2px 8px">
  Beta
</MCTag>

components.name: MCBoxTab
components.description: Box-style tab components for segmented tab navigation. Consists of MCBoxTabsWrapper, MCBoxTabs, MCBoxTab, and MCBoxTabIndicator styled components.
whenToUse: Box-style segmented tab navigation with underline indicator
When MCBarTabs is too minimal and a boxed tab style is needed
doNotUse: For standard bar-style tabs — use MCBarTabs
For page-level navigation — use routing

components.name: MCCollapse
components.description: Animated collapse/expand wrapper. Re-exports Material UI Collapse component.
whenToUse: Animated show/hide of content sections
When MCAccordion is too opinionated and you need raw collapse animation
doNotUse: For accordion pattern with header — use MCAccordion instead
For dialog/modal animations — use MCCommonDialog

components.name: MCMarkdown
components.description: Renders markdown text as HTML using the Remarkable library.
whenToUse: Rendering markdown-formatted text content
Help text, documentation, or user-authored content with markdown formatting
doNotUse: For plain text display — use styled-components directly
For rich text editing — this is display-only

components.name: MCChip
components.description: Removable chip/tag component for displaying selected options with optional remove action.
whenToUse: Displaying selected options as removable chips
Tag-style display of multi-select values
Building custom chip-based select inputs
doNotUse: For non-removable labels — use MCTag
For status badges — use MCStatus
components.example:
<MCChip
  option={{ value: 'us', label: 'United States', key: 'us' }}
  onRemove={(opt) => handleRemove(opt)}
/>

components.name: MCDataTable
components.description: Virtualized data table with column reordering, resizing, frozen columns, and custom cell renderers. For high-performance tabular data.
whenToUse: High-performance tables with many rows requiring virtualization
Tables needing column reordering, resizing, or frozen columns
When MCReportTable does not provide enough customization
doNotUse: For standard list pages — use MCReportTable (higher-level API)
For simple data display — use a basic HTML table or MCReportTable

components.name: MCSelect
components.description: Standalone select dropdown (not Formik-integrated). Built on react-select.
whenToUse: Standalone select inputs outside Formik context
Filter dropdowns, toolbar selects, and other non-form selects
doNotUse: Inside Formik forms — use MCFormSingleRichSelect or MCFormMultiRichSelect
For fewer than 3 options — use radio buttons
components.example:
<MCSelect
  options={[{ label: 'Active', value: 'active' }, { label: 'Paused', value: 'paused' }]}
  value={selectedOption}
  onChange={setSelectedOption}
  label="Status"
/>

components.name: MCDatePicker
components.description: Standalone date picker input. Not Formik-integrated.
whenToUse: Standalone date selection outside Formik context
Filter bars, toolbar date selectors
doNotUse: Inside Formik forms — use MCFormDateRangePicker or MCFormDateTimeRangePicker
For date+time selection — use MCTimePicker alongside or a datetime component
components.example:
<MCDatePicker
  value={startDate}
  onChange={setStartDate}
  placeholder="Select date"
  minDate={new Date()}
/>

components.name: MCTimePicker
components.description: Standalone time picker input. Not Formik-integrated.
whenToUse: Standalone time selection outside Formik context
Paired with MCDatePicker for date+time selection
doNotUse: Inside Formik forms — use MCFormDateTimeRangePicker

components.name: MCDynamicDropdown
components.description: Async multi-select dropdown with paginated option loading, search, and popper-based positioning.
whenToUse: Multi-select dropdowns with server-side option loading
When options are too many to load at once (paginated/cursor-based)
Async search-as-you-type select inputs
doNotUse: For static option lists — use MCSelect or MCFormSingleRichSelect
Inside Formik forms with static options — use MCFormMultiRichSelect
components.example:
<MCDynamicDropdown<string>
  loadOptions={async ({ cursor, searchText, limit }) => {
    const result = await fetchOptions({ cursor, searchText, limit });
    return { options: result.items, cursor: result.nextCursor, totalCount: result.total };
  }}
  value={selectedOptions}
  onChange={setSelectedOptions}
  selectPlaceholder="Select items..."
/>

components.name: MCFilter
components.description: Multi-dimension filter component supporting number, string, option, and single-option filter types with an apply button.
whenToUse: Table/list filtering with multiple dimensions
When users need to build complex filter queries across multiple fields
Data explorer-style filtering UIs
doNotUse: For simple single-field search — use MCSearchBar
For form-based filtering — use Formik form with MCFormSingleRichSelect

components.name: MCModal
components.description: Low-level modal wrapper. Re-exports react-modal. For most use cases, prefer MCCommonDialog or MCConfirmDialog.
whenToUse: Building custom modal experiences not covered by MCCommonDialog
When full control over modal content, styling, and behavior is needed
doNotUse: For standard dialogs — use MCCommonDialog
For confirmation dialogs — use MCConfirmDialog
For simple overlays — use MCPopover

components.name: MCPopper
components.description: Low-level popper positioning component. Re-exports Material UI Popper.
whenToUse: Building custom positioned overlays (tooltips, dropdowns, menus)
When MCPopover does not provide enough positioning control
doNotUse: For standard popover/menu — use MCPopover or MCMoreActionsButton
For modal dialogs — use MCCommonDialog

components.name: MCStateIcon
components.description: State-specific icon component for entity lifecycle states. Different from MCIcon — renders state-specific colored icons.
whenToUse: Rendering colored state icons for entity lifecycle (active, paused, draft, etc.)
Paired with MCStatus or used standalone in table cells
doNotUse: For general-purpose icons — use MCIcon
For full status badge with label — use MCStatus

components.name: MCSingleNumberInput
components.description: Standalone number input with formatting, prefix/suffix, and validation messages. Uses react-number-format internally.
whenToUse: Standalone number inputs outside Formik context
Formatted number entry with prefix/suffix (currency, percentage)
Number inputs with inline error/warning/guide messages
doNotUse: Inside Formik forms — use MCFormNumberInput
components.example:
<MCSingleNumberInput
  value={budget}
  onValueChange={({ floatValue }) => setBudget(floatValue)}
  prefix="$"
  placeholder="Enter budget"
/>

components.name: MCDebounceInput
components.description: Generic debounced input wrapper. Wraps any input element with debounce behavior.
whenToUse: Adding debounce to any input or textarea element
When you need to delay onChange callbacks to reduce API calls
doNotUse: For search — use MCSearchBar which has built-in debounce
When immediate feedback is needed — debounce adds delay
